nodes.name: security.py

nodes.languageNotes: Implements defense-in-depth: binary denylist, flag allow/block lists, protected directory guards, rm target validation, and input/output path sandboxing.

nodes.name: _get_policy

nodes.name: is_denied

nodes.name: server.py

nodes.name: create_server

nodes.name: discovery.py

nodes.name: __init__.py

nodes.name: hijacklibs.py
nodes.summary: Imports HijackLibs (DLL hijacking vulnerability) YAML data into a SQLite database, providing structured lookup of Windows DLL sideloading and search-order hijacking attack vectors with normalized path handling.

nodes.name: lolbas.py
nodes.summary: Imports LOLBAS (Living Off The Land Binaries and Scripts) definitions from YAML into a SQLite database, cataloging ~227 Windows built-in tools with their abuse functions, expected paths, MITRE ATT&CK mappings, and detection guidance.

nodes.name: loldrivers.py
nodes.summary: Imports LOLDrivers (Living Off The Land Drivers) YAML definitions into a SQLite database, collecting known vulnerable and malicious Windows kernel drivers with filename, hash (SHA256/SHA1/MD5), Authentihash, and certificate metadata for detection.

nodes.name: process_expectations.py

nodes.name: import_hijacklibs

nodes.name: _parse_hijacklib_yaml
nodes.summary: Parses a single HijackLibs YAML file: extracts DLL name, description, hijack type, expected path, and iterates vulnerable executables with their paths and SHA256 hashes, producing structured entries with normalized path variables.

nodes.name: _normalize_path_var
nodes.summary: Normalizes Windows path variables in DLL/EXE paths (e.g., %SYSTEM32% to C:\\Windows\\System32), applying expansion tables for known variables, drive letters, and filesystem paths, producing consistent lowercase backslash-separated paths for reliable matching.

nodes.name: get_hijack_types
nodes.summary: Returns the canonical list of recognized DLL hijacking types: Phantom, Sideloading, Search Order, and Environment Variable, used for query and validation reference.

nodes.name: import_lolbas
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the full LOLBAS import pipeline: creates the lolbas table, iterates category directories (OSBinaries, OSLibraries, OSScripts, OtherMSBinaries), parses each YAML via _parse_lolbas_yml, and inserts LOLBin entries with abuse functions, MITRE techniques, and detection guidance serialized as JSON arrays.

nodes.name: _parse_lolbas_yml
nodes.summary: Parses a single LOLBAS YAML file: extracts filename, abuse function names and their MITRE ATT&CK technique IDs, expected file paths, detection guidance entries, and resource URLs, returning a structured dictionary for database insertion.

nodes.name: get_lolbin_functions
nodes.summary: Returns the full catalog of recognized LOLBAS abuse function categories (Download, Execute, AWL Bypass, UAC Bypass, Credentials, Encode, Decode, etc.) for use in lookups and investigative queries.

nodes.name: import_loldrivers
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the full LOLDrivers import pipeline: creates tables for known-vulnerable and known-malicious drivers, iterates YAML files, delegates to _parse_loldriver_yaml, filters by category (vulnerable/malicious), and inserts driver records with filename, hashes, signed status, cert info, and sample metadata.

nodes.name: _parse_loldriver_yaml
nodes.summary: Parses a single LOLDriver YAML file: extracts driver metadata including resource names (with regex-based driver name extraction), sample hashes (SHA256/SHA1/MD5), Authentihash values, signed status, file info, and creation dates, returning a structured dictionary with nested sample and signature data.

nodes.name: load_process_expectations

nodes.name: import_process_expectations

nodes.name: get_process_tree

nodes.name: get_user_processes

nodes.summary: OpenCTI worker replicas (2 instances) processing background enrichment, import, and export jobs for the OpenCTI platform.

nodes.name: connector-mitre
nodes.summary: OpenCTI connector that ingests the MITRE ATT&CK knowledge base (tactics, techniques, threat actors, malware) every 7 days into the local OpenCTI platform.

nodes.name: connector-cisa-kev
nodes.summary: OpenCTI connector that ingests the CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog daily into the local OpenCTI platform for vulnerability awareness.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project overview and entry-point documentation for AgentIR, an autonomous incident response system on a SIFT workstation. Covers the problem statement, key differentiators (cryptographic evidence integrity, Solana-anchored ledger, human-in-the-loop requirements, OS hardening), architecture and security control summaries, documentation index, and running instructions.

nodes.name: architecture.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive architecture documentation for AgentIR describing the four-component system (sift-gateway, case-dashboard examiner portal, agentir-core, and FastMCP stdio backends), two request lifecycles (agent tool call and examiner evidence seal), data-at-rest layout, and deployment model.

nodes.name: dfir-hardening-guide.md
nodes.summary: Detailed DFIR operating system hardening guide covering four layered mechanisms: chattr +i immutable flag, auditd kernel audit rules, AppArmor MAC profile enforcement, and inotify evidence watcher. Includes threat coverage scenarios (accidental overwrite, deliberate tampering, gateway RCE), limitation analysis, and a maintenance checklist for operators.

nodes.name: evidence-chain-of-custody.md
nodes.summary: Evidence chain of custody specification describing four integrity layers: cryptographic chain (per-file SHA-256, evidence manifest, evidence ledger), operational controls (portal-only sealing, two-tier evidence gate, evidence diff), OS-level hardening (chattr, auditd, AppArmor), and Solana blockchain on-chain anchoring with setup and verification procedures.

nodes.name: security-controls.md
nodes.summary: Security controls catalog for AgentIR covering eight control domains: transport security (TLS 1.2+ with per-installation CA), authentication (bearer tokens, PBKDF2 portal password, domain-separated key derivation), authorization (RBAC, password guard, session security), agent containment (no direct shell, evidence gate, response guard), evidence chain of custody, OS-level hardening, audit trail infrastructure, and report integrity with control traceability.

nodes.name: tool-audit-2026-05-25.md
nodes.summary: Audit of 79+ MCP tools across 8 backend servers (case-mcp, forensic-mcp, sift-mcp, opensearch-mcp, forensic-rag-mcp, opencti-mcp, windows-triage-mcp, report-mcp) as of 2026-05-25. Includes tool counts and purposes per backend, summary statistics, an interaction map of cross-backend relationships, and critical issues blocking fully autonomous DFIR operations.

nodes.name: auth_log.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines the Linux authentication log artifact (/var/log/auth.log, /var/log/secure): a PAM-based log recording login attempts, sudo usage, SSH events, and account changes. Includes proven facts, common misinterpretations, corroboration guidance, and cross-MCP checks for threat intel lookups.

nodes.name: bash_history.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines the Linux shell history artifact (.bash_history, .zsh_history): records interactive commands entered by users, with notes on timestamp availability, history disabling, and attacker evasion techniques. Includes corroboration guidance linking to auth_log, syslog, and wtmp.

nodes.name: crontab.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines the Linux cron jobs artifact: per-user and system-wide crontab files used for scheduled task execution and persistence detection. Covers Debian and Red Hat path conventions, systemd timer alternatives, and MITRE ATT&CK T1053.003 mapping.

nodes.name: proc_filesystem.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines the Linux /proc filesystem artifact: a virtual filesystem exposing kernel and process information critical for live system analysis. Documents key proc entries (cmdline, exe, environ, fd, maps, net) and warns that /proc is volatile and not collectible from disk.

nodes.name: ssh_artifacts.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines the Linux SSH artifacts: authorized_keys for persistence detection, known_hosts for lateral movement tracing, sshd_config for backdoor configurations, and per-user SSH client config. Addresses MITRE ATT&CK T1098.004 (SSH Authorized Keys).

nodes.name: syslog.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines the Linux syslog artifact (/var/log/syslog, /var/log/messages): central system log aggregating kernel, service, and application messages. Covers Debian and Red Hat path differences, timestamp caveats (missing year/timezone), and log rotation implications.

nodes.name: systemd_journal.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines the Linux systemd journal artifact: a binary structured logging system with rich metadata fields (__REALTIME_TIMESTAMP, __MONOTONIC_TIMESTAMP). Distinguishes persistent (/var/log/journal) vs volatile (/run/log/journal) storage and clarifies journal-vs-syslog relationship.

nodes.name: wtmp_utmp.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines the Linux login records artifacts (wtmp, utmp, btmp, lastlog): binary files tracking user sessions, login/logout times, boot events, and failed login attempts. Documents the tools required to read each format (last, lastb, who, w, lastlog).

nodes.name: activitiescache.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines Windows ActivitiesCache database forensic artifact knowledge, covering timeline activity tracking, application usage records, and file access pattern evidence scope, timestamp fields, and common misinterpretations.

nodes.name: alternate_data_streams.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines NTFS Alternate Data Streams (ADS) forensic artifact knowledge, covering detection methods for hidden data in the filesystem, evidence capabilities, and corroboration strategies.

nodes.name: amcache.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines Windows Amcache registry forensic artifact knowledge, covering application compatibility cache in Amcache.hve, SHA1 hash records, and investigation query patterns including cross-host hash correlation.

nodes.name: bam.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines Windows Background Activity Moderator (BAM) forensic artifact knowledge, recording application execution paths, last-run timestamps, and corroboration with Prefetch and Amcache artifacts.

nodes.name: bits_jobs.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines BITS jobs forensic artifact knowledge, covering Background Intelligent Transfer Service job persistence, file transfer records, and detection of potential malware abuse.

nodes.name: browser_history.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines browser history forensic artifact knowledge, covering multi-browser (Chrome/Firefox/Edge) URL visits, download records, timestamps, and user activity timeline reconstruction.

nodes.name: defender_quarantine.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines Windows Defender Quarantine forensic artifact knowledge, covering quarantined file locations, metadata encoding, timestamps, and correlation with original detection events.

nodes.name: digital_signatures.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines digital signatures forensic artifact knowledge, covering Authenticode signature verification for executables, certificate chain analysis, and signature timestamp forensic significance.

nodes.name: event_logs_defender.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines Windows Defender event log forensic artifact knowledge, covering anti-malware scan events, detection results, and corroboration with quarantine artifacts.

nodes.name: event_logs_firewall.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines Windows Firewall event log forensic artifact knowledge, covering network connection activity, filter rule changes, and inbound/outbound traffic event analysis.

nodes.name: event_logs_network_share.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines network share event log forensic artifact knowledge, covering shared resource access, permission change events, and lateral movement detection analysis strategies.

nodes.name: event_logs_powershell.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines PowerShell event log forensic artifact knowledge, covering script execution records, module loading, remote command activity, and script block logging analysis fields.

nodes.name: event_logs_rdp.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines RDP event log forensic artifact knowledge, covering remote desktop connection events, session activity, network source addresses, and RDP client configuration analysis.

nodes.name: event_logs_security.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines Windows Security event log forensic artifact knowledge, covering authentication events (4624/4625), privilege usage, process creation (4688), and audit policy configuration with 6 investigation pattern templates.

nodes.name: event_logs_services.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines services event log forensic artifact knowledge, covering Windows service installation (7045), start/stop events, and detection of service-based persistence or lateral movement.

nodes.name: event_logs_sysmon.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines Sysmon event log forensic artifact knowledge, covering process creation (Event 1), network connections (Event 3), file creation (Event 11), and registry modification (Event 12/13) with investigation patterns.

nodes.name: event_logs_system.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines Windows System event log forensic artifact knowledge, covering service failures, driver loads, system time changes, and hardware-related events.

nodes.name: event_logs_task_scheduler.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines Task Scheduler event log forensic artifact knowledge, covering scheduled task creation (4698), modification, execution, and deletion events for persistence and lateral movement detection.

nodes.name: event_logs_wmi.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines WMI event log forensic artifact knowledge, covering WMI activity records, persistence events (EventFilter/EventConsumer bindings), and remote WMI invocation detection.

nodes.name: hayabusa_alerts.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines Hayabusa alerts forensic artifact knowledge, covering Sigma rule-based detection results, alert level classification (critical/high/medium/low), MITRE technique mapping, and cross-host correlation investigation patterns.

nodes.name: jump_lists.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic knowledge entry for Windows Jump Lists, describing what they prove (application usage, file access paths, timestamps), their limitations, timestamp interpretation, common misinterpretations, related tools (JLECmd), and cross-MCP validation checks.

nodes.name: lnk_files.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic knowledge entry for Windows LNK (shortcut) files, documenting locations in Recent and Office folders, what they prove (file access, target metadata, timestamps, MAC addresses), their limitations, common misinterpretations, and corroboration with Jump Lists, Prefetch, and MFT.

nodes.name: mft.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic knowledge entry for the NTFS Master File Table, the most comprehensive file metadata artifact on NTFS volumes. Documents SI vs FN timestamp analysis, timestomping detection patterns (SI vs FN, SI vs Shimcache, SI vs PE compile time), ADS detection, Zone.Identifier downloaded-code detection, and cross-artifact correlation with Prefetch and Shimcache.

nodes.name: mountpoints2.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic knowledge entry for the MountPoints2 registry key, which tracks all mounted volumes including USB devices, network shares, and removable media per user. Documents what it proves (device presence, UNC paths, last connection time) and corroboration with Shellbags and LNK files.

nodes.name: ntfs_i30.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic knowledge entry for NTFS I30 directory index entries, which contain file name, size, and timestamps for files within directories. Documents how index slack space retains deleted file entries, I30 vs SI timestamp comparison for timestomping detection, and parsing tools (MFTECmd, INDXParse.py).

nodes.name: ntfs_logfile.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic knowledge entry for the NTFS LogFile, the transaction journal that records file system metadata operations (create, delete, rename, attribute changes). Documents its circular buffer limitations (~64MB), LSN-based relative ordering, timestomping detection via transaction records, and the known gap that it is not yet parsed by the platform.

nodes.name: ntfs_resident_data.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic knowledge entry for MFT resident data, where small file contents (under ~700-900 bytes) are stored directly in the MFT entry DATA attribute. Documents recovery of deleted small file contents from un-reallocated MFT entries, relevant for recovering attacker scripts and configuration files.

nodes.name: powershell_transcripts.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic knowledge entry for PowerShell Transcript logs, documenting exact commands and output from PowerShell sessions when transcription is enabled via Start-Transcript or group policy. Distinguishes transcript logs from ScriptBlock logging (Event ID 4104) as complementary mechanisms.

nodes.name: prefetch.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic knowledge entry for Windows Prefetch files, the primary execution artifact proving application execution count, first/last run times, and files referenced during first 10 seconds. Includes investigation patterns for first-time execution detection, 32-bit payload detection via WOW64, anti-forensics detection via MFT cross-reference, and scripting engine frequency anomalies.

nodes.name: psreadline.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic knowledge entry for PSReadLine command history, capturing all interactive PowerShell console commands in a plain text file. Documents its limitations (no output, no per-line timestamps, no scripted commands) and corroboration with PowerShell event logs and transcript files.

nodes.name: rdp_bitmap_cache.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic knowledge entry for RDP Bitmap Cache, containing visual fragments of remote desktop sessions. Documents cache file locations (BMC and BIN formats), what screens the user saw during RDP sessions, reconstruction tools (bmc-tools), and corroboration with RDP event logs for lateral movement investigation.

nodes.name: recycle_bin.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic knowledge entry for the Windows Recycle Bin, documenting I (metadata: original path, deletion time, size) and R (file data) files per user SID. Clarifies that only Explorer-based deletions use the Recycle Bin, while command-line and Shift+Delete bypass it, and that emptying does not guarantee data destruction.

nodes.name: registry_run_keys.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic knowledge entry for Registry Run and RunOnce keys, the most common Windows persistence mechanism. Documents 6+ Run key locations including HKCU, HKLM, Wow6432Node, and Policies paths. Includes investigation patterns for scripting in autostart, unusual service paths, WDigest credential config, and LSA security package injection.

nodes.name: registry_services.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic knowledge entry for Windows service configuration in the SYSTEM registry hive. Documents service registration, start types, ImagePath analysis, kernel driver vs user-mode service distinction, and investigation patterns for unusual binary paths, command-line arguments in services, and recently modified service keys.

nodes.name: scheduled_tasks.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic knowledge entry for Windows Scheduled Tasks, documenting XML definitions, registry TaskCache, and task metadata. Includes investigation patterns for LOLBins in scheduled tasks, encoded PowerShell detection, and tasks executing binaries from non-standard paths. Covers COM handler stealth, hidden tasks, and registry persistence after XML deletion.

nodes.name: shellbags.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic knowledge entry for Windows ShellBags, recording folder access and view preferences per user in BagMRU and Bags registry keys across both NTUSER.DAT and UsrClass.dat hives. Documents ShellBags persistence even after folder deletion, making them excellent historical evidence of directory navigation including USB devices and network shares.

nodes.name: shimcache.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic knowledge entry for ShimCache (AppCompatCache), documenting file presence on the system. Clarifies that on Windows 8+, ShimCache proves file existence only (execution flag removed), and includes investigation patterns for unknown executables in system directories, cross-host binary presence indicating lateral movement, and executables in unusual staging paths.

nodes.name: srum.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic knowledge entry for SRUM (System Resource Usage Monitor), an ESE database tracking application resource consumption including network bytes sent/received per application, CPU time, and user SID attribution. Documents its 1-hour aggregation intervals and that it records total network data but not destination IPs.

nodes.name: ual.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic knowledge entry for User Access Logging (UAL), a Windows Server feature logging client access to server roles and services. Documents that UAL is server-only (Server 2012+), provides aggregated access counts and first/last seen timestamps rather than session-level detail, and its relevance for tracking lateral movement source IPs.

nodes.name: userassist.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic knowledge entry for UserAssist registry keys, recording GUI-based program execution per user with launch count, last execution time, and focus time. Documents ROT13 encoding of values, the limitation to Explorer-launched programs only, and corroboration with Prefetch, BAM, and Amcache for command-line and service execution context.

nodes.name: usn_journal.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic artifact knowledge entry for the NTFS Update Sequence Number (USN) Journal, detailing file system change event tracking across NTFS volumes. Includes 8 investigation query patterns for detecting deleted prefetch, executable deployment, anti-forensics deletion, SDelete traces, and lateral movement via cross-host file deployment.

nodes.name: volatility_memory.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic artifact knowledge entry for Volatility 3 RAM dump analysis, covering 8 investigation patterns including process tree anomaly detection, code injection (malfind), hidden process enumeration (psscan), network connection analysis (netscan), and DLL injection detection (ldrmodules).

nodes.name: volume_shadow_copies.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic artifact knowledge entry for Volume Shadow Copy Service (VSS) point-in-time NTFS snapshots. Documents evidentiary value for file recovery, common misconceptions about snapshot availability and deletion, and cross-references to USN Journal and MFT for timeline corroboration.

nodes.name: wer.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic artifact knowledge entry for Windows Error Reporting (WER) crash dumps and reports. Highlights that WER captures crashes from any application including malware, and minidumps may contain strings, URLs, and encryption keys useful as forensic artifacts.

nodes.name: wmi_persistence.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic artifact knowledge entry for WMI permanent event subscriptions used as a fileless persistence mechanism. Covers five consumer types (CommandLine, ActiveScript, LogFile, NTEventLog, SMTP), MITRE ATT&CK T1546.003 mapping, and cross-references to Sysmon Event IDs 19/20/21 for detection.

nodes.name: anti_patterns.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines 6 common cognitive and analytical anti-patterns in forensic investigation (premature attribution, confirmation bias, cascade error, tool trust, temporal assumption, scope creep), with severity levels and avoidance guidance for investigator self-review.

nodes.name: checkpoints.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines 4 gate-check rules for forensic action types (attribution, root cause, exclusion, clean declaration), specifying minimum audit IDs, required evidence types, and human approval requirements per action.

nodes.name: confidence.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines 4 evidence confidence levels (HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW, SPECULATIVE) with assessment criteria and minimum required audit IDs, providing a consistent framework for classifying finding reliability.

nodes.name: evidence_standards.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines 5 evidence quality tiers (CONFIRMED, INDICATED, INFERRED, UNKNOWN, CONTRADICTED) with level descriptions and minimum independent source counts for consistent evidence quality assessment.

nodes.name: evidence_template.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines the standard field template for forensic evidence entries, specifying required field types for title, audit IDs, raw artifacts, observation, interpretation, confidence, and finding type.

nodes.name: rules.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines 7 forensic discipline rules (FD-001 through FD-007) governing evidence-chain referencing, human approval, autonomous attribution prohibition, UNKNOWN neutrality, confidence justification, premature exclusion avoidance, and pre-escalation corroboration — the core rulebook for forensic investigation methodology.

nodes.name: event_logs.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic collection checklist for Windows Event Logs, specifying key .evtx log paths (Security, System, Application, PowerShell, Sysmon, TaskScheduler, TerminalServices) and recommended capture tools including KAPE, wevtutil.exe, and FTK Imager.

nodes.name: filesystem.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic collection checklist for filesystem artifacts including NTFS metadata structures ($MFT, USN Journal, $LogFile), Prefetch, Amcache, and SRUM, with KAPE, FTK Imager, and Sleuth Kit as recommended tools.

nodes.name: memory.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic collection checklist for volatile memory images, emphasizing capture-before-shutdown priority and listing WinPmem, DumpIt, Magnet RAM Capture, and AVML as recommended acquisition tools.

nodes.name: registry.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic collection checklist for Windows Registry hives (SYSTEM, SOFTWARE, SAM, SECURITY, DEFAULT, NTUSER.DAT, UsrClass.dat), with guidance on also capturing transaction logs (.LOG1/.LOG2) for dirty hive recovery.

nodes.name: corroboration.yaml
nodes.summary: DFIR investigation playbook defining corroboration checks for 8 artifact categories (persistence, lateral movement, malware, credential access, suspicious binary, suspicious document, process injection, unknown execution), each mapped to specific forensic tools and evidence sources with rationale.
nodes.languageNotes: YAML-based DFIR playbook mapping artifact categories to tool-specific corroboration checks with rationale for each step.

nodes.name: false_positives.yaml
nodes.summary: Documents common false positive scenarios for windows-triage baseline checks (unknown_file and unexpected_parent), listing benign causes such as enterprise agents and distinguishing factors for analysts.

nodes.name: tool_interpretation.yaml
nodes.summary: Interprets scores and verdicts for 4 forensic tools (check_file, check_process_tree, forensic-rag search, opencti search_threat_intel), documenting what each score means, common false positives, and reliability caveats.
nodes.languageNotes: YAML-based score interpretation reference mapping forensic tool verdicts and scores to their operational meaning, with per-tool false positive and caveat sections.

nodes.name: credential_access.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines a multi-phase investigation playbook for credential theft, dumping, and Kerberos abuse incidents (MITRE T1003, T1558). Guides analysts through LSASS access detection, tool identification, scope assessment, and impact analysis across Identify, Tool Identification, Scope, Impact, and Record phases.

nodes.name: data_staging.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines an investigation playbook for data collection and staging activity indicating exfiltration preparation (MITRE T1074, T1560). Covers archive tool execution detection, content analysis via Shellbags and Jump Lists, network exfiltration checks, and USB device correlation across five phases.

nodes.name: dns_anomaly.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines an investigation playbook for DNS tunneling, domain generation algorithms (DGA), and unusual query patterns (MITRE T1071.004, T1568.002). Guides DNS log analysis, DGA classification, threat intelligence lookup, and source system identification across five phases.

nodes.name: email_compromise.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines an investigation playbook for email account takeover, mailbox rule manipulation, and business email compromise (MITRE T1114, T1534). Covers sign-in analysis, mailbox rule and OAuth permission review, content examination, and financial impact assessment across five phases.

nodes.name: log_tampering.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines an investigation playbook for event log clearing, suspicious gaps, and anti-forensic activity targeting log integrity (MITRE T1070.001, T1070). Guides detection of log clearing events, gap timeline reconstruction, alternative source consultation, and perpetrator attribution across five phases.

nodes.name: malware_analysis.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines an investigation playbook for suspicious executable, document, and script analysis during forensic triage. Covers detection via sigcheck/densityscout/YARA, context collection from Prefetch/Amcache/ShimCache, static analysis via REMnux, scope assessment, and findings documentation across five phases.

nodes.name: powershell_anomaly.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines an investigation playbook for suspicious PowerShell activity including encoded commands, download cradles, and obfuscated scripts (MITRE T1059.001). Guides PowerShell log parsing, Base64 decoding, command de-obfuscation, technique analysis, and impact assessment across five phases.

nodes.name: remote_access.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines an investigation playbook for unexpected remote access including RDP, PsExec, WMI, and WinRM connections (MITRE T1021, T1570). Covers access method identification, source analysis, post-access execution context, and lateral movement chain mapping across five phases.

nodes.name: suspicious_autorun.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines an investigation playbook for unexpected persistence mechanisms including registry autoruns, services, scheduled tasks, WMI subscriptions, and BITS jobs (MITRE T1547, T1053, T1543). Covers persistence identification, baseline validation, binary analysis, and timeline correlation across five phases.

nodes.name: suspicious_connection.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines an investigation playbook for anomalous network connections including beaconing, unusual ports, and known-bad destinations (MITRE T1071, T1573). Covers PCAP/Zeek analysis, threat intelligence correlation, process-to-connection mapping, and payload extraction across five phases.

nodes.name: suspicious_execution.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines an investigation playbook for unknown or unexpected program execution from forensic artifacts (MITRE T1204, T1059). Covers Prefetch/Amcache/Shimcache parsing, filename and LOLBin validation, threat intelligence lookup, REMnux static analysis, and lateral scope assessment across six phases.

nodes.name: timestomping.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines an investigation playbook for timestamp manipulation in NTFS file system metadata (MITRE T1070.006). Covers MFT $SI vs $FN timestamp comparison, nanosecond precision anomaly detection, USN Journal cross-referencing, and timestomping tool identification across five phases.

nodes.name: unusual_logon.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines an investigation playbook for unexpected authentication events indicating unauthorized access or credential misuse (MITRE T1078, T1021). Covers logon event parsing, account baseline comparison, process/file context collection, lateral movement checking, and threat intelligence corroboration across six phases.

nodes.name: usb_activity.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines an investigation playbook for USB device connections and removable media activity related to data theft or malware delivery (MITRE T1052.001, T1091). Covers USB registry artifact parsing, connection timeline correlation, file operation analysis via LNK/Jump List/MFT, and cross-system device tracking across five phases.

nodes.name: 7z.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines the forensic tool knowledge entry for 7z, a multi-format archive extractor supporting 7z, zip, gzip, tar, rar and other formats, with caveats around extraction safety, exit codes mapping, and investigation workflow guidance for forensic use.

nodes.name: bulk_extractor.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines the forensic tool knowledge entry for bulk_extractor, a high-performance record carving tool that extracts emails, URLs, credit cards, EXIF data, and Windows artifacts from disk images and unallocated space, with scanner selection guidance, output field explanations, and investigation sequence.

nodes.name: exiftool.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines the forensic tool knowledge entry for ExifTool, a metadata extraction tool for documents, images, PDFs, executables, and archives, with guidance on GPS coordinate recovery, PE binary analysis, author attribution, and JSON/CSV structured output for automated forensic processing.

nodes.name: dc3dd.yaml
nodes.summary: Knowledge base entry for dc3dd, a DoD-patched variant of dd that performs forensic disk imaging with inline hashing (md5/sha1/sha256), verification, and optional split output for raw format acquisition.

nodes.name: ewfacquire.yaml
nodes.summary: Knowledge base entry for ewfacquire, a libewf-based tool that acquires forensic images in Expert Witness Format (E01/EWF) with compression, embedded case metadata, and segment-based integrity verification.

nodes.name: ewfmount.yaml
nodes.summary: Knowledge base entry for ewfmount, a FUSE-based tool that mounts E01/EWF forensic images as virtual raw device files, enabling transparent read-only access by downstream analysis tools.

nodes.name: vshadowinfo.yaml
nodes.summary: Knowledge base entry for vshadowinfo, a libvshadow tool that enumerates Volume Shadow Copy (VSS) snapshots on NTFS volumes, listing snapshot GUIDs and creation timestamps for forensic timeline analysis.

nodes.name: vshadowmount.yaml
nodes.summary: Knowledge base entry for vshadowmount, a FUSE-based tool that mounts VSS snapshots as virtual device files for read-only file system comparison, enabling recovery of deleted or pre-encryption files.

nodes.name: 1768_cobalt.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic tool knowledge entry for 1768.py, a Python-based Cobalt Strike beacon configuration extractor that recovers C2 server addresses, sleep timers, watermarks, and other IOC-rich configuration data from beacon binaries.

nodes.name: capa.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic tool knowledge entry for CAPA, a static analysis framework that detects behavioral capabilities in PE/ELF executables using a rule-based framework mapped to MITRE ATT&CK and Malware Behavior Catalog techniques.

nodes.name: densityscout.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic tool knowledge entry for densityscout, a triage utility that calculates byte density (entropy) of files to identify potentially packed, encrypted, or compressed executables warranting deeper analysis.

nodes.name: hollows_hunter.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic tool knowledge entry for HollowsHunter, a live Windows process scanner using the PE-sieve engine to detect process hollowing, code injection, replaced PE images, and in-memory patches.

nodes.name: maldump.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic tool knowledge entry for maldump, a Python utility that extracts quarantined malware samples from antivirus quarantine directories (Defender, Kaspersky, McAfee, Symantec) along with associated metadata.

nodes.name: moneta.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic tool knowledge entry for Moneta, a live Windows memory scanner that detects malicious memory modifications including reflective DLL injection, manual mapping, and shellcode through memory region analysis.

nodes.name: sigcheck.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic tool knowledge entry for sigcheck, the Sysinternals utility that verifies digital signatures, catalogs PE file metadata, computes multiple hash types (MD5/SHA1/SHA256/imphash), and optionally queries VirusTotal for detection ratios.

nodes.name: strings_tool.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic tool knowledge entry for strings, a passive binary analysis utility that extracts printable ASCII and Unicode character sequences from files for initial malware triage, IOC extraction, and embedded indicator discovery.

nodes.name: yara.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic tool knowledge entry for YARA, the industry-standard pattern matching engine for malware identification and classification using community-curated and custom rule-based signatures across files, memory dumps, and live processes.

nodes.name: check_file.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines the check_file MCP tool metadata for validating Windows file paths against known-good baseline databases via the windows-triage-mcp server, including caveats, output format, and investigation sequence guidance.

nodes.name: check_process_tree.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines the check_process_tree MCP tool metadata for validating Windows process parent-child relationships against expected baselines via the windows-triage-mcp server, with advisories and investigation workflow steps.

nodes.name: search.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines the search MCP tool metadata for semantic search across the forensic knowledge base (Sigma rules, MITRE ATT&CK, Atomic Red Team) via the forensic-rag-mcp server, including relevance score interpretation and result filtering guidance.

nodes.name: search_threat_intel.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines the search_threat_intel MCP tool metadata for querying the OpenCTI threat intelligence platform for indicators, threat actors, malware families, TTPs, and CVEs via the opencti-mcp server, with TLP handling caveats and entity relationship guidance.

nodes.name: dumpit.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic knowledge definition for DumpIt, Comae Technologies' one-click Windows memory acquisition tool designed for field deployment by non-technical responders, producing raw or DMP format memory dumps.

nodes.name: memprocfs.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic knowledge definition for MemProcFS, a cross-platform memory analysis framework that presents memory as a virtual filesystem for intuitive navigation, supporting both live analysis and offline memory image examination.

nodes.name: winpmem.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic knowledge definition for WinPmem, a Windows physical memory acquisition tool that uses a kernel driver to produce raw-format forensic memory images for Volatility-based analysis.

nodes.name: blkls.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic knowledge entry for TSK blkls — extracts unallocated disk clusters from images for file carving and slack space analysis. Documents caveats, output format, exit codes, advisories, quick-start usage, and a multi-step investigation sequence.

nodes.name: fls.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic knowledge entry for TSK fls — lists files and directories in a disk image including deleted entries with MAC timestamps. Includes field meanings, bodyfile output guidance for mactime timeline generation, and cross-tool integration with mmls and icat.

nodes.name: icat.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic knowledge entry for TSK icat — extracts file contents from disk images by inode or MFT entry number. Covers NTFS alternate data stream access, deleted file recovery procedures, and chain-of-custody hashing guidance.

nodes.name: mmls.yaml
nodes.summary: Forensic knowledge entry for TSK mmls — displays partition table layout (GPT, MBR, BSD, Sun, Mac) of a disk image, showing offsets, sizes, and types. Serves as the prerequisite step for all other TSK tools that require partition offsets.

nodes.name: hayabusa.yaml
nodes.summary: Describes the Hayabusa tool -- a Rust-based Windows event log fast forensics timeline generator with Sigma-based detection rules and MITRE ATT&CK mapping. Documents caveats, output schema for CSV/JSON, exit codes, advisories, quick-start commands, multi-step investigation sequence, and field meanings for 13 output columns including RuleTitle, Level, MitreTactics, and MitreTechniques.

nodes.name: mactime.yaml
nodes.summary: Describes the mactime tool -- a Sleuth Kit Perl script that converts pipe-delimited bodyfile format (from fls, MFTECmd, etc.) into human-readable chronological timelines with MACB timestamp indicators. Documents bodyfile format requirements, caveats, comma-delimited output options, date-range filtering advisories, and a multi-step investigation workflow linking file system timelines to event log and application timelines.

nodes.name: plaso.yaml
nodes.summary: Describes the Plaso/log2timeline engine -- a Python-based super-timeline generator that extracts timestamps from multiple artifact types into a unified Plaso storage file for later post-processing with psort. Documents processing caveats (high memory usage, long runtimes), parser selection strategies, output format, exit codes, advisories including Timesketch integration, and an investigation sequence with cross-tool references to Hayabusa and Volume Shadow Copy recovery.

nodes.name: psort.yaml
nodes.summary: Describes the psort tool -- a Python companion to log2timeline that filters, sorts, and exports Plaso storage files to various output formats including l2tcsv, dynamic, json_line, and xlsx. Documents caveats about input dependencies, output format selection, ISO 8601 time-slicing, Plaso filter syntax, and an investigation workflow covering timezone conversion and event-type review.

nodes.name: appcompat_processor.yaml
nodes.summary: Tool definition for AppCompatProcessor, a cross-system ShimCache/AmCache correlation utility used to detect lateral movement and execution patterns across Windows hosts in forensic investigations.

nodes.name: cylr.yaml
nodes.summary: Tool definition for CyLR, a cross-platform forensic artifact collector for rapid triage of Windows, Linux, and macOS systems, producing ZIP archives of collected evidence.

nodes.name: kape.yaml
nodes.summary: Tool definition for KAPE (Kroll Artifact Parser and Extractor), a Windows-only automated forensic collection and processing tool that combines target-based collection with module-based parsing.

nodes.name: amcacheparser.yaml
nodes.summary: Knowledge base entry for AmcacheParser, a Zimmerman tool that parses Amcache.hve registry hives to extract file presence records, SHA1 hashes, and timestamps from the Windows Application Compatibility Cache.

nodes.name: appcompatcacheparser.yaml
nodes.summary: Knowledge base entry for AppCompatCacheParser, a Zimmerman tool that parses the Application Compatibility Cache (ShimCache) from the SYSTEM registry hive to determine file presence on Windows systems.

nodes.name: bstrings.yaml
nodes.summary: Knowledge base entry for bstrings, a Zimmerman tool that extracts strings from binary files with regex pattern matching, Unicode support, and byte-offset reporting for forensic string analysis.

nodes.name: evtxecmd.yaml
nodes.summary: Knowledge base entry for EvtxECmd, a Zimmerman tool that parses Windows Event Log files (.evtx) with map support for structured field extraction across Security, System, PowerShell, and Sysmon logs.

nodes.name: jlecmd.yaml
nodes.summary: Knowledge base entry for JLECmd, a Zimmerman tool that parses Windows Jump List files (automatic and custom destinations) to reveal recently and frequently accessed files per application.

nodes.name: lecmd.yaml
nodes.summary: Knowledge base entry for LECmd, a Zimmerman tool that parses Windows LNK shortcut files to extract target file metadata, MAC timestamps, and network path information for forensic timeline construction.

nodes.name: mftecmd.yaml
nodes.summary: Knowledge base entry for MFTECmd, a Zimmerman tool that parses NTFS $MFT, $UsnJrnl, $J, $Boot, and $SDS files to extract file system metadata, detect timestomping via $SI/$FN timestamp comparison, and reconstruct file activity timelines.

nodes.name: pecmd.yaml
nodes.summary: Knowledge base entry for PECmd, a Zimmerman tool that parses Windows Prefetch files (.pf) to determine program execution history, including run count, last run time, and files loaded during execution. Requires Windows for decompression.

nodes.name: rbcmd.yaml
nodes.summary: Knowledge base entry for RBCmd, a Zimmerman tool that parses Windows Recycle Bin $I files to recover original file paths, file sizes, and deletion timestamps for evidence of file deletion activity.

nodes.name: recmd.yaml
nodes.summary: Knowledge base entry for RECmd, a Zimmerman Registry Explorer command-line tool for batch processing registry hives with plugin support for extracting UserAssist, BAM, Run Keys, services, and ShellBags artifacts.

nodes.name: sbecmd.yaml
nodes.summary: Knowledge base entry for SBECmd, a Zimmerman tool that parses ShellBags from NTUSER.DAT and UsrClass.dat registry hives to reconstruct folder browsing and access history in Windows Explorer.

nodes.name: sqlecmd.yaml
nodes.summary: Knowledge base entry for SQLECmd, a Zimmerman tool that parses SQLite databases using configurable maps for extracting browser history, downloads, and other application data from Chrome, Firefox, and Edge databases.

nodes.name: srumecmd.yaml
nodes.summary: Knowledge base entry for SrumECmd, a Zimmerman tool that parses the System Resource Usage Monitor (SRUM) ESE database for application execution, network usage (bytes sent/received), and energy consumption history over 30-60 day retention. Requires Windows.

nodes.name: wxtcmd.yaml
nodes.summary: Knowledge base entry for WxTCmd, a Zimmerman tool that parses the Windows Timeline ActivitiesCache.db SQLite database to extract user activity tracking records from Windows 10 1803+ systems.

nodes.name: sleuthkit.yaml

tour.title: Project Overview: What is AgentIR?
tour.description: AgentIR is a production-grade runtime that lets an AI agent (Hermes) autonomously investigate digital forensics incidents on a SIFT Workstation VM. The README lays out the problem statement, the four-component architecture (sift-gateway, case-dashboard, agentir-core, and eight MCP backend servers), and the core guarantees: cryptographic auditability, chain-of-custody controls, and human-in-the-loop oversight. The architecture document deepens this with service diagrams, tool-surface maps, and inter-service communication patterns.

tour.title: Gateway: HTTPS Entry Point and Backend Fabric
tour.description: The sift-gateway is the system's front door. Its CLI entry point loads configuration, validates TLS, and launches an ASGI server via uvicorn. The Gateway class in server.py manages the lifecycle of all eight MCP backend processes -- starting them lazily on first use, reaping idle instances, and building a deduplicated tool-map from every backend. The REST layer exposes Starlette routes for tool discovery, invocation, and backend health management. Behind the scenes, the backends subsystem uses a factory pattern to dispatch to either STDIO subprocess backends (with sandboxed environment injection) or HTTP/StreamableHTTP backends (with pinned TLS certificate factories), all behind a common abstract MCPBackend interface.
tour.languageLesson: Python asyncio with FastMCP: Backend lifecycle uses asyncio.create_subprocess_exec for STDIO backends with idle timeout callbacks. The abstract base class defines a start/stop/health interface that both backend types implement, enabling the gateway to treat them uniformly via the factory in backends/__init__.py -- a classic Strategy pattern.

tour.title: Core Foundation: Evidence Integrity, Case I/O, and Approval Auth
tour.description: Building on the gateway's request routing, the agentir-core shared library provides the cryptographic and case-management primitives that every MCP backend depends on. The evidence_chain module implements manifest-based file registration, sealing, HMAC verification, and immutable flag setting -- ensuring every piece of evidence carries a provable chain of custody. The case_io module handles all case file operations: loading and saving findings, timeline entries, TODOs, IOCs, evidence registries, and approval logs. The approval_auth module adds analyst authentication with PBKDF2 hashing, lockout protection, and HMAC key derivation -- the cryptographic foundation for the human-in-the-loop approval workflow that gates every destructive action.
tour.languageLesson: PBKDF2 key derivation: The approval_auth module uses PBKDF2 (Password-Based Key Derivation Function 2) with a high iteration count to hash analyst passwords. This slows brute-force attacks by making each guess computationally expensive. The derived key is then used in an HMAC (Hash-based Message Authentication Code) to sign approval tokens -- ensuring that approvals cannot be forged even if an attacker gains read access to the case file.

tour.title: Evidence Indexing Engine: OpenSearch MCP
tour.description: The opensearch-mcp backend is the heaviest of the eight MCP servers, exposing 25+ tools for forensic evidence ingestion and search. Its server.py registers tools for ingesting evidence in multiple formats (auto-detection for E01/VHDX/raw containers, JSON/JSONL, CSV/TSV, access logs, and memory images via Volatility), querying indexed data with OpenSearch query_string syntax, and managing the indexing lifecycle. The discover.py module auto-scans evidence directories to detect artifact types, log formats, and host mappings. The bulk.py engine handles high-volume indexing with adaptive batching, circuit-breaker protection, and retry logic. The ingest_cli.py provides the CLI interface for dry-run previews and full ingestion runs. This is the pipeline that transforms raw evidence bytes into searchable, analyzable records.
tour.languageLesson: Circuit breaker pattern: The bulk.py engine implements a circuit breaker that monitors failure rates during bulk indexing. When error rates exceed a threshold, the breaker trips to 'open' state, halting further indexing to prevent cascading failures. After a cooldown period, it transitions to 'half-open' to test recovery before fully resetting -- the same pattern used in production service meshes for fault isolation.

tour.title: Investigation Workflow: Forensic MCP and Case MCP
tour.description: Once evidence is indexed (Step 4), the forensic-mcp and case-mcp servers drive the investigation workflow. Forensic MCP registers the core investigation tools: record_finding (staging DRAFT findings with observations, interpretations, confidence levels, audit IDs, MITRE mappings, and IOCs), record_timeline_event (staging timestamped events linked to findings), and query_case for reading back the timeline and action log. Case MCP adds case lifecycle management: case_status for phase detection (ORIENT, SEALED, INGESTING, INGESTED, TRIAGE, FINDINGS, REPORTING), evidence_list for inventory, evidence_verify for integrity checks, and set_case_metadata for incident classification. Together they form the human-review workflow -- the AI proposes findings and timeline events as DRAFT, and the examiner approves or rejects each one through the dashboard (Step 9).
tour.languageLesson: Findings lifecycle pattern: Each finding passes through DRAFT -> APPROVED or REJECTED states. The audit_ids field links every finding back to the specific tool invocations that produced the evidence, creating a cryptographic provenance chain. This is what distinguishes AgentIR from a simple AI chat -- every conclusion is traceable back to actual forensic tool output with HMAC-verified integrity.

tour.title: Analysis Pipeline: SIFT MCP and Windows Triage MCP
tour.description: These two MCP servers are the analytical engines. SIFT MCP wraps the SIFT workstation's forensic tool suite: list_available_tools catalogs installed tools by category, get_tool_help fetches manpages and usage guidance, check_tools verifies availability, suggest_tools recommends tools for specific artifact types, and run_command executes tools with audit-trail logging. The catalog.py file (the 4th-highest fan-in node in the codebase) provides the canonical tool registry that all other modules reference. Windows Triage MCP provides baseline validation for Windows artifacts: check_artifact compares files against known-good path/hash baselines, check_system validates services and scheduled tasks against OS-version profiles, and check_registry cross-references registry entries against the 12GB known-good registry database. The tightly-coupled db/ subsystem (known_good.py, schemas.py, context.py, registry.py) manages the SQLite baseline databases that power these comparisons.
tour.languageLesson: SQLite for forensic baselines: The windows-triage DB subsystem uses multiple SQLite databases (known_good.db, known_good_registry.db, context.db) stored at ~12GB. SQLite is ideal here because it requires no server process, supports concurrent reads, and the databases are read-only during analysis -- the forensic workstation downloads updated baselines periodically but never modifies them during an investigation, maintaining evidence integrity.

tour.title: Forensic Knowledge Base and Intelligence Enrichment
tour.description: The forensic-knowledge package is the structured intelligence backbone, with loader.py as the single highest fan-in node in the entire codebase (imported by 21 other modules). It reads a YAML knowledge tree covering Windows and Linux artifacts (Prefetch, MFT, Amcache, event logs, Sysmon, syslog, systemd journals), forensic tool definitions (Zimmerman tools, Volatility, Hayabusa), investigation playbooks, and evidence standards. The forensic-rag-mcp server builds on this with semantic search across 23K+ incident response knowledge records (Sigma rules, MITRE ATT&CK, Atomic Red Team, Splunk Security content). The opencti-mcp server connects to an OpenCTI threat intelligence platform for IOC lookups, threat actor mapping, and campaign correlation. Together they transform raw forensic data into intelligence-enriched findings with MITRE technique tags and confidence scores.
tour.languageLesson: YAML as a knowledge representation format: The forensic-knowledge package uses YAML files (not JSON or a database) for artifact definitions, playbooks, and tool metadata. YAML's support for comments, anchors/aliases, and multi-line strings makes it ideal for human-authored forensic knowledge that needs to be both machine-queryable and human-readable. The loader.py caches parsed YAML in memory with lazy loading per category for performance.

tour.title: Report Generation: From Findings to Narrative
tour.description: After findings are approved (Step 5) and enriched with intelligence (Step 7), the report-mcp server transforms them into structured narratives. It exposes tools for managing case metadata (incident type, severity, TLP classification, affected systems), generating reports with multiple profiles (full, executive, timeline, IOC, findings, status), and persisting rendered output to the case's reports/ directory. The report profiles map to the Zeltser Incident Response Writing framework, ensuring outputs follow industry-standard report structures. This is the final analytical step before an examiner presents conclusions -- the AI drafts a complete report, the examiner reviews and approves it through the dashboard.

tour.title: Examiner Portal: The Human-in-the-Loop Dashboard
tour.description: The case-dashboard is the web UI where human examiners maintain oversight. Its routes.py file is the single highest fan-out node in the entire codebase (134 outgoing edges), exposing 50+ API endpoints for every aspect of case management: viewing evidence inventories, reviewing and approving/rejecting DRAFT findings, inspecting timeline events, managing investigation TODOs, checking evidence chain integrity, and generating reports. The dashboard is the primary interface for the approval workflow described in Step 5 -- every AI-proposed finding and timeline event appears here for human judgment before it becomes part of the official case record. This is the UI that enforces the 'human-in-the-loop' guarantee central to AgentIR's design.
tour.languageLesson: FastAPI route organization: The routes.py file uses FastAPI's router pattern with dependency injection for shared services (case directory resolution, auth checks). The 50+ endpoints are organized by domain (evidence, findings, timeline, TODOs, reports) with consistent request/response models leveraging Pydantic for validation. This keeps the route layer thin -- each endpoint delegates to the core library from Step 3 for business logic.

tour.title: Deployment and Security Architecture
tour.description: The final step covers how AgentIR gets deployed and hardened on a SIFT Workstation VM. The install.sh script is a hardened, idempotent, zero-argument installer that provisions the complete runtime: Python environment with uv, all 12 workspace packages, configuration scaffolding, and system integration. The docker-compose.yml orchestrates the core services (OpenSearch, the gateway, and optionally the full OpenCTI platform with Redis, RabbitMQ, MinIO, and connectors for MITRE and CISA KEV). The systemd unit file runs the gateway as a persistent background service with automatic restart. On the security side, the evidence-chain-of-custody document specifies four integrity layers (cryptographic chain, immutable flags, audit logging, HMAC verification), and the security-controls catalog covers eight control domains including TLS 1.2+ transport security, AppArmor MAC confinement, kernel auditd rules, and chattr +i immutable protection.
tour.languageLesson: Defense in depth for forensic systems: AgentIR applies four independent integrity layers: (1) HMAC chain on every evidence file so modification is cryptographically detectable, (2) chattr +i immutable flag at the filesystem level preventing even root from modifying sealed evidence, (3) auditd kernel rules logging every file access attempt, and (4) AppArmor mandatory access control confining the gateway process to only authorized paths and capabilities. If any single layer fails, the others still protect the evidence.
